V1.claim: An agent answers codebase questions with fewer tokens when Cartogate's tools are available than when it must read/grep the source.
V1.notes: Token counts are exact (API usage field); means ± stdev over trials. Model output is non-deterministic — re-run for fresh draws.
V1.title: Token usage (live agent, with vs. without Cartogate)

V10.claim: check_duplicate latency stays flat as the codebase grows (indexed O(1) lookup).
V10.notes: A grep/scan baseline grows linearly with the tree; the indexed gate does not.
V10.title: Scaling (flat gate latency)

V2.claim: Warm graph queries answer in well under a millisecond — far faster than scanning the source tree, and orders under the 50ms in-loop SLO.
V2.notes: Index time is off the gate's latency budget (warm-resident store), per the SLO design.
V2.title: Query latency (graph vs grep)

V3.claim: Against pyright's references on a real repo, Cartogate matches with far higher precision than name-grep; its recall gap is the re-exports/inferred receivers it soundly skips.
V3.notes: Micro-averaged over scored symbols. File granularity (Cartogate returns referencing symbols, pyright returns occurrences).
V3.title: Navigation accuracy (find_references vs pyright oracle)

V3_fixture.claim: Cartogate recovers the true referencing files with higher precision than name-grep.
V3_fixture.notes: Grep false-positives: helpers.py (comment) and billing.py (a different symbol).
V3_fixture.title: Navigation accuracy (find_references vs grep) — illustrative fixture

V3_go_fixture.claim: On Go, Cartogate recovers the true referencing files more precisely than name-grep.
V3_go_fixture.notes: Grep false-positive: billing.go (a different Validate symbol).
V3_go_fixture.title: Navigation accuracy (find_references vs grep) — Go fixture

V3_jmespath.claim: Against pyright's references on a real repo, Cartogate matches with far higher precision than name-grep; its recall gap is the re-exports/inferred receivers it soundly skips.
V3_jmespath.notes: Micro-averaged over scored symbols. File granularity (Cartogate returns referencing symbols, pyright returns occurrences).
V3_jmespath.title: Navigation accuracy (find_references vs pyright oracle)

V4.claim: On objectively-labeled cases built from real symbols, the gate catches true duplicates without false-flagging different-signature or method-named functions; name-grep over-blocks both.

V4.notes: Labels are objective by construction (extra-param and method cases are not top-level duplicates). Census reports real signature collisions.
V4.title: Duplicate detection (check_duplicate vs name-grep, real symbols)

V4_fixture.claim: Catches real duplicates (incl. annotation-only differences) without false-flagging same-named methods or different-signature functions.

cases.note: same callable with annotations/return type — a textual compare would miss it
name exists (billing.charge) but the signature differs — not the same callable
`close` exists only as User.close (a method) — not a top-level duplicate

V4_fixture.notes: Grep false-positives: charge (different params) and close (a method).
V4_fixture.title: Duplicate detection (check_duplicate vs name-grep) — illustrative fixture

V4_go_fixture.claim: On Go, catches a real top-level duplicate by signature without false-flagging a same-named function with a different parameter list.

cases.note: name exists (auth + billing) but the 2-arg signature matches neither — not a duplicate
cases.signature: func Validate(x int, y int) bool
func Charge(amount int, currency string) bool

V4_go_fixture.notes: Grep over-blocks `Validate(x,y)` — the name exists but no top-level callable has that signature.
V4_go_fixture.title: Duplicate detection (check_duplicate vs name-grep) — Go fixture

V4_jmespath.claim: On objectively-labeled cases built from real symbols, the gate catches true duplicates without false-flagging different-signature or method-named functions; name-grep over-blocks both.
V4_jmespath.notes: Labels are objective by construction (extra-param and method cases are not top-level duplicates). Census reports real signature collisions.
V4_jmespath.title: Duplicate detection (check_duplicate vs name-grep, real symbols)

V5_fixture.claim: Flags breaking signature/visibility changes to an exported symbol; passes safe ones.

metric.baseline: none (no standard tool detects contract breaks automatically)

cases.label: add-param
cases.note: added a required parameter — callers break

cases.label: narrow-visibility
cases.note: exported symbol made internal — importers break

cases.label: annotate-only
cases.note: same parameters, only annotations added — safe

V5_fixture.notes: Baseline is manual code review; Cartogate makes the check deterministic.
V5_fixture.title: Contract-break detection (check_contract) — illustrative fixture

V6_fixture.claim: Flags docs explicitly documenting a symbol with higher precision than grepping markdown.
V6_fixture.notes: Conservative matching (exact qname or unique bare name) is what wins precision; ambiguous bare names are skipped (a known recall limitation).
V6_fixture.title: Doc-drift detection (doc_drift vs md-grep) — illustrative fixture

V6_go_fixture.claim: On Go, flags the doc that explicitly documents a symbol with higher precision than grepping markdown.
V6_go_fixture.notes: Grep false-positive: guide.md (a prose mention, not a code reference).
V6_go_fixture.title: Doc-drift detection (doc_drift vs md-grep) — Go fixture

V7.claim: When suggest_tests names a test, coverage confirms that test really does exercise the symbol (high precision). Overall recall against full runtime coverage is bounded by how much of that coverage is statically reachable at all: Cartogate recovers ≈all of the statically-reachable tests, and the rest is dynamic dispatch no sound static analyzer can follow.

metric.pytest_summary: 1 failed, 588 passed, 21 skipped, 1 xfailed, 1 warning in 3.10s

V7.notes: Truth = tests whose coverage executed the symbol's lines (transitive). Overall recall is low where coverage is dominated by dynamic dispatch (getattr tables, runner.invoke): only ~61% of these symbols' runtime coverage is statically reachable, but Cartogate recovers ~7% of that reachable set.
V7.title: Test selection (suggest_tests vs coverage oracle)

V7_fixture.claim: Selects the tests exercising a change — run fewer tests, lose no relevant coverage.
V7_fixture.notes: On this fixture, 1 of 3 tests is relevant — a 67% reduction with full relevant recall.
V7_fixture.title: Test selection (suggest_tests) — illustrative fixture (direct unit tests)

V7_go_fixture.claim: On Go, selects exactly the test that exercises a changed symbol.
V7_go_fixture.notes: TestAuthenticate exercises Authenticate, not Validate, so it is correctly excluded.
V7_go_fixture.title: Test selection (suggest_tests) — Go fixture

V7_jmespath.claim: When suggest_tests names a test, coverage confirms that test really does exercise the symbol (high precision). Overall recall against full runtime coverage is bounded by how much of that coverage is statically reachable at all: Cartogate recovers ≈all of the statically-reachable tests, and the rest is dynamic dispatch no sound static analyzer can follow.
V7_jmespath.notes: Truth = tests whose coverage executed the symbol's lines (transitive). Overall recall is low where coverage is dominated by dynamic dispatch (getattr tables, runner.invoke): only ~6% of these symbols' runtime coverage is statically reachable, but Cartogate recovers ~88% of that reachable set.
V7_jmespath.title: Test selection (suggest_tests vs coverage oracle)

V8.claim: Same input yields byte-identical tool output across runs and across processes (different PYTHONHASHSEED).
V8.notes: No model, blake2b ids (unseeded), explicit output sorting → reproducible.
V8.title: Determinism (byte-identical across processes)

V9.claim: A BLOCK always rests on EXTRACTED, top-level evidence: INFERRED facts never gate, and method-name collisions across classes are never false-blocked.
V9.notes: On Cartogate's own source, top-level scoping excludes method-name collisions that a naive signature index would false-block.
V9.title: Soundness (R7 + top-level scoping)
